title: BMW M3 Touring poised for epic Neue Klasse comeback
description:
Massive success of BMW's straight-six super estate lays groundwork for a second outing

The M3 Touring will live on in BMW's Neue Klasse era, in part thanks to unexpectedly high demand in markets such as the US and China.
While the M3 saloon is now in its sixth generation, the performance model has only been offered in estate form since the arrival of the G80-generation version in 2021. But strong sales since then have prompted BMW to bring the super-estate back with the next-generation M3, as previewed recently with the Vision M Neue Klasse concept.
The current model is powered by a 523bhp twin-turbo 3.0-litre straight six, and a variant of that engine is set to be offered in the next-gen car, alongside a quad-motor pure-electric version.
BMW has already previewed a new electric 3 Series Touring and, unlike rival Mercedes-Benz, it has pledged to offer EV and ICE versions of the standard car in estate form - and BMW M sales boss Sylvia Neubauer said the performance version will remain.
"The M3 Touring is one of the best models that we have in our portfolio," Neubauer told Autocar.

"Normally China is not an estate market at all, but we decided to bring the Touring to China only in the M3 version. So you can't buy a regular 3 Series Touring there but the M3 Touring is a lifestyle statement. And it's been a huge success in China, and the rest of the world."
Neubauer noted that the M3 Touring has also been a hit in the US, another market where they don't offer the standard estate because "it's a market for SUVs and very big cars".
She added that demand for the model was largely organic. "Our dealers are so happy to have this car, because they don't have to push it," she said.
"There's demand, and they're asking for more and more production, so we've been increasing the volume."

title: Audi Q4 E-Tron
description:
Big-selling electric SUV looks to boost its well-established appeal

Audi managed something many of its competitors couldn’t last year. It hit a 28% share of electric vehicle sales in the UK, 4% north of the average and enough to meet the government’s target. The Audi Q4 E-tron played a pivotal role in the brand achieving that milestone, as Britain’s third-best-selling electric car and Audi’s second-best-selling model behind the A3.Whether rumours of a softening of EV sales targets come to fruition or not, carmakers need these things to sell. Thus, after five years on sale, Audi’s most popular pure plug-in car has received an update.

title: Driven: Porsche Taycan gets gearshifts - and renewed driver appeal
description:
Zuffenhausen takes on Hyundai's N division with its own take on a virtual gearbox – and we drive it

Porsche is rolling out a number of major updates to its Taycan sports saloon, most notably a new E-Shift virtual gearbox that has been designed to inject an extra dose of feedback on road and track. 
The new virtual gearbox is not dissimilar to that used by Hyundai’s N models and is effectively a simulated eight-speed transmission. Porsche created the system by taking the characteristics of its dual-clutch PDK gearbox and mapping them into the Taycan’s ECU.
Engineers then had to marry up the virtual gearbox with the Taycan’s physical two-speed automatic in order for the system to work seamlessly.

Crucially, Porsche claims that running in E-Shift mode doesn’t limit the Tayan’s power output, nor does it impact its real-world range and efficiency. While Hyundai’s system is tied exclusively to N-badged derivatives, Porsche is rolling out its new E-Shift virtual gearbox across the Taycan’s entire line-up, with each model variant given its own characteristics.
The new E-Shift gearbox works in unison with Porsche Electric Sound, an evolution of the previous engine note available with the Taycan. As Porsche sound design engineer Max Geiger explained, while the team experimented with mimicking a petrol engine, they opted for a synthesised engine note to match the Taycan’s electric roots. 
So what is a Taycan E-Shift like to drive? Thumb the E-Shift button on the GT sport steering wheel and the instrument cluster displays a rev counter in the centre of the dash, with the virtual ’box defaulting to automatic. You’ll immediately notice an audible change inside the car, as the Porsche Electric Sound 'idles' much like that of an combustion engine. 

In Normal mode, it acts just like a smooth eight-speed PDK. Even in this relaxed setting, the simulated ratios immediately give the Taycan more personality and I could see myself using this even for mundane motorway stints.
Flick the rotary E-Shift button to the right and this puts the car into manual mode, allowing you to virtually shift gears using the paddles on the steering wheel. The gearchanges are seamless and the level of integration is of a very high standard.
Shift too late and, like a PDK 911, the rev needle will bounce off the fake limiter. Stamp on the throttle in a high gear – say, seventh – and the Taycan is sluggish and won’t give you that whipcrack performance. You have to flick it down a few gears before you can deploy all the performance. 
Switch the Taycan to Sport or Sport Plus and the gearchanges are far more pronounced, with a discernible kick through your seat as you climb up through the gears. Our GTS’s virtual gearbox didn’t feel as hardcore as Hyundai’s N e-Shift, but the more subtle vibrations and jolts from the gearbox align more with the Taycan’s pseudo-GT character.
That said, I imagine the 1093bhp Taycan Turbo GT with E-Shift would feel more extreme and involving on a track. Ultimately, the ability to control the shifts both into and out of a corner gives you a greater sense of your road speed, allowing you to better judge how you drive the car down a certain road and establish a proper rhythm. 

The new E-Shift function is a reasonable £750 option, but this being Porsche, there’s a catch. You need the GT sports steering wheel, Sports Chrono package and Bose surround sound system.
Opt for a lower-rung model, which forgoes these options as standard, and the total price for adding the E-Shift comes to just over £3000. These features come as standard on the GTS and Turbo S, hence the additional cost of E-Shift is lower – or you can go all-out on a Turbo GT and get the simulated ’box as standard. 
Given that the Taycan was already an incredibly dynamic and engaging electric sports car, the addition of a virtual gearbox has unlocked a whole new layer of involvement. If you like driving but want an electric performance car, there are only a handful of EVs that will offer as much fun as a Taycan E-Shift. 
Porsche Taycan GTS E-ShiftNew virtual gearbox makes one of the most dynamic electric sports cars on sale even more complete as a driver’s car. 
Price £119,015 (£750 for E-Shift) Engine Two permanent magnet synchronous motor Power 690bhp Torque 583lb ft Gearbox 1-spd reduction gear (front), 2-spd automatic (rear) Kerb weight 2285kg0-62mph 3.3sec Top speed 155mph Battery 105kWh (total) Range, economy 389 miles, 3.4mpkWh CO2, tax band 0g/km, 4% Rivals Audi E-tron GT, Polestar 5

title: What if you drove an Autocar? The name that's more than a magazine
description:
The Autocar companies: The auto pioneers, pretenders, and partners who shared our name

Like 'Volkswagen Golf' or 'Citroën', 'Autocar' is an odd name normalised in the public consciousness purely by familiarity.
Like old André Citroën, this magazine (and website) has a valid excuse: while his grandfather had sold lemons, we were predicting what would become the common name for an entirely new invention.
Plenty of people setting up in the 1890s named their endeavours the This, That or The Other Autocar Company, and indeed one was so presumptive as to definitively call his The Autocar Company. He was well-born engineer Louis Semple Clarke, who produced his first car, The Pittsburgher, in 1897 with help from his brothers and their father.
In late 1899, the 32-year-old gave "quite an enthusiastic welcome" to Henry Sturmey, founding editor of The Autocar - a magazine of which he was apparently a "close student".
Sturmey was on a mission from Coventry to survey the incipient American car industry, and from Pennsylvania's industrial capital he reported: "The factory is at present situated on the fourth floor of a tall building, but I was informed that a new factory is now being erected to cover some three and a half acres of ground, and being laid out for an estimated output of 10 carriages per day!" - as if Clarke's ambition hadn't already been demonstrated by $1 million in founding capital (that's roughly £30m today).

Sturmey was treated to a ride in a 4hp, two-cylinder, two-seat runabout-type car "presenting a very neat and handy appearance" and set to cost a very reasonable $500 pre-tax (say £15,000 now). He found it "handled over very bad roads excellently" and "ran very smoothly and satisfactorily, though one of the tyres showed a great inclination to get flat". Recent history made Sturmey doubt that The Autocar Co could hit that low price target, but should it succeed, he felt it would "certainly be producing the cheapest satisfactory car on the market". 
By 1911, The Autocar Co was no longer making cars - but not due to the bankruptcy that befell most 'brass era' firms, rather because it found far greater prosperity as the first American truck maker, or so it claims. Yes, claims present tense, because it still exists as a maker of 'severe-duty' trucks in Alabama.
'Autocar' was already an outdated term by the early 1950s, yet that was what an Israeli duo picked for their fledgling nation's first car company.
Autocars Ltd started out making plastic-bodied vans, named Sussita and created by British minnow Reliant. Then, in 1960, co-founder Yitzhak Shubinsky visited London for the Racing and Sports Car Show, where he saw a novel chassis and a quirky plastic kit car body in close proximity to each other. Combining them, he reckoned, could produce a fine roadster for American export.

Having done the required deals, Shubinsky again engaged Reliant to make his idea a reality - and after significant chassis revisions and agreement to buy some 1.7-litre four-cylinder engines from Ford, the result was the Autocars Sabra. Or, if you were British, the Reliant Sabre, Tamworth making a version themselves under their own name. Notwithstanding some criticism of its suspension, our road testers concluded: "It has an acceptable performance, is fundamentally safe, fun to drive and economical to run."
Come 1963, we found Autocars' Haifa plant running at an annual production rate of a little over 2000, having just introduced the Carmel saloon - "the first purely Israeli passenger car", we said, although in hindsight it looks awfully like a Reliant Regal with a fourth wheel.
Enjoy full access to the complete Autocar archive at the magazineshop.com
"Since 1956 the [Autocars] project has gathered momentum quickly," we continued. "A local maker of steel furniture has taken on the job of making chassis frames, while other local manufacturers have been able to supply glass, trim materials, upholstery and road springs."
Further models arrived through the decade, borrowing parts from Triumph and Japan's Hino, but the Autocars name would last only until 1970 and the firm would fold in 1980.
It had made many thousands of cars by then - although very few of them had found homes in America.

title: जब एयरपोर्ट पर स्पॉट हुए रश्मिका मंदाना तो सेल्फी लेने के लिए लग गई लोगों की भीड़
description: रश्मिका मंदाना पैन इंडिया स्टार बन चुकी हैं. पिछले कम वक्त में उन्होंने कई ब्लॉकबस्टर फिल्में दी हैं, जिसमें पुष्पा 2, छावा और एनिमल का नाम शामिल है. अब धनुष के साथ कुबेर में दिखेंगी, लेकिन फिल्म की रिलीज से पहले जब रश्मिका मंदाना एयरपोर्ट पर स्पॉट हुई तो लोग उनके साथ सेल्फी लेने लगे। इस दौरान एक्ट्रेस ने किसी को भी मायूस नहीं किया सबके साथ क्यूट स्माइल के साथ सेल्फी क्लिक कराई। लुक की बात करें तो एक्ट्रेस ने डार्क ब्लू जींस के साथ व्हाइट टॉप के ऊपर ब्लैक ब्लेजर कैरी किया जिसमें रश्मिका मंदाना बेहद कमाल लगीं।

title: रवीना टंडन की लाडली राशा थडानी का स्टाइलिश अंदाज, ब्लैक आउटफिट में लूटी महफिल
description: 90 के दशक में अपनी खूबसूरती और दमदार एक्टिंग से बॉलीवुड पर राज करने वाली रवीना टंडन की बेटी राशा थडानी भी अब एंटरटेनमेंट की दुनिया में अपनी पहचान बना रही हैं। अपने डेब्यू के बाद से ही राशा को फैंस का खूब प्यार मिल रहा है। उनकी क्यूट स्माइल और शानदार डांसिंग स्किल्स ने उन्हें कम समय में ही काफी पॉपुलर बना दिया है। हाल ही में राशा एक अवॉर्ड इवेंट में नजर आईं, जहां उनके ग्लैमरस लुक ने हर किसी का ध्यान खींचा। राशा ने इस खास मौके के लिए ब्लैक कलर का स्टाइलिश आउटफिट चुना, जो उन्हें बेहद एलीगेंट लुक दे रहा था। उनका यह लुक न सिर्फ क्लासी था, बल्कि इसमें मॉडर्न टच भी था, जो उनकी पर्सनालिटी को बखूबी कॉम्प्लिमेंट कर रहा था।  इस दौरान राशा हैवी मेकअप के साथ खुले बालों में नजर आई। उनके इस लुक पर फैंस ने खूब प्यार बरसाया।

title: ‘Confidence is Not a Feeling’: Educator Shobhit Nirwan Explains Why
description: Shobhit Nirwan, through his recent LinkedIn post, explains that confidence is not something that is found before one begins the work, but rather develops gradually with time when the individual chooses not to give up, regardless of the outcome or mood.
content: It is very common for students to have self-doubts and face uncertainties while achieving their goals. Educator and content creator Shobhit Nirwan, through a recent LinkedIn post, has shared a message for those students and young professionals who often hesitate before taking the first step, and stressed the issue that everyone is waiting to feel ready, prepared, and confident.According to him, most individuals waste their time waiting to feel confident, prepared, and certain enough before taking the first step towards their goal. This waiting might appear as a responsible and practical approach, but in reality, it points towards being stuck in the same place.'Confidence is Not a Feeling You Find Before Work'In his post, he explains something that nobody usually says: confidence is not a feeling one can find before work or after achieving a goal. Rather, it is a feeling that shows up because of the work. As he writes, “Confidence is not a feeling you find before the work; it is a feeling that shows up because of the work.”Confidence shows up in times when individuals, every single time, show up even on the days when they don't feel like it, keep moving ahead towards their goals regardless of the outcome, and choose not to quit on the days when quitting feels like the most reasonable decision.These are the moments and the decisions of the individuals that actually help in building confidence. Further, he says, the confidence is not built in the good days when everything is working, and applause is being received from all around for one’s work, but on the days that are ordinary and yet one does their thing anyway. This helps in building confidence and genuine self-belief. According to him, the people would look at those individuals and think that they are so sure of themselves. The reason behind them being so sure of themselves is that they just stopped waiting to feel ready and started to build proof they could.He concludes his post as he writes, “You will not find confidence before you beginYou will only find it because you did not stop!” He contradicts the myth about confidence that comes on its own and says that one can only find it when they stop giving up and continue moving ahead, regardless of the mood or outcome.(Disclaimer – This article summarises a post by Shobhit Nirwan. While Times Now has verified the public data presented, the interpretations of 'intent' remain those of the creator. This content is for educational purposes and does not substitute professional medical/nutritional advice.)

title: Rajasthan Staff Selection Board Plans Tablet-Based Exam Model to Prevent Paper Leaks
description: The RSSB is implementing new strategies to combat paper leaks in examinations. Chairman Alok Raj discussed transitioning to tablet-based tests, citing challenges in the traditional examination process.
content: At a time when paper leaks are emerging as a significant challenge in conducting consequential tests, the Rajasthan Staff Selection Board (RSSB) is working on new initiatives to prevent paper leaks. In an exclusive conversation with Times Now Navbharat, the RSSB Chairman Alok Raj said that the board is considering tablet-based tests as an alternative to conventional mediums.Elucidating the obstacles being faced by the organising authorities, he said that preparing an examination paper, getting it printed, and then delivering it from districts to examinees is a tedious task. “Thousands of people are a part of this entire process, and if someone makes a mistake or has ill intentions, there’s always a risk of unauthorised disclosure,” he added.A lucid solution, according to Raj, is a computer-based test; however, the state doesn’t have adequate laboratories to support large-scale computer-based examinations. In addition, the majority of labs aren’t guarded enough for conducting seamless competitive examinations.Moreover, computer-based tests carry a risk of remote access and the possibility of control falling into the hands of exam mafias.Dry Run Held in Presence of Hackers and Vendors for Tablet-Based TestTherefore, the state is exploring a more viable alternative, i.e., a tablet-based test. According to Raj, a dry run has already been carried out in the presence of hackers and vendors.The board is further developing the system with the assistance of IIT Kanpur and IIT Madras. “Our experiment is still underway, and we are close to completing it,” said Alok Raj.In this format, tablets will be handed out to the candidates, and the question paper will be delivered via a server, while the answer sheets will be in physical form. Notably, candidates will be able to sit for the test in public-owned schools only.“This examination will follow a hybrid model,” Raj said, adding that uploading question papers from the server to the tablets will be a risky task, as mafias and hackers may try to trespass to acquire the paper. However, research to address this challenge is ongoing. “IIT Madras is assisting us with the issue; if successful, it will bring a major revolution,” he added.He further said that the introduction of tablet-based tests will shorten the duration of paper printing and transportation. The possibility of the paper setter leaking would be ruled out with the help of a question bank vault. “A question bank will be developed so that leaks become impossible,” he concluded.

title: RSOS Result 2026 Declared at rsosadmission.rajasthan.gov.in
description: RSOS result 2026 for Classes 10 and 12 has been announced. Candidates can now access their scorecards using their login credentials on the official website.
content: The Rajasthan State Open School (RSOS), Jaipur, has declared the Class 10 and 12 results. Students who appeared for the exams can check their scores and download their mark sheets from the official website, rsosadmission.rajasthan.gov.in, using their respective login credentials, which include enrollment number and date of birth.RSOS conducted the Class 10 and 12 open school examinations from April 23 to May 14, 2026, and April 23 to May 20, 2026, respectively.How to Check RSOS Result 2026The students can follow the steps given below to download the RSOS 2026 marksheet:Visit the official website, rsosadmission.rajasthan.gov.in. On the homepage, look for the result link and click on it.Enter the enrollment number and date of birth.Click on the 'Submit' button.The RSOS Result 2026 will be displayed on the screen.Download and save it for future reference.The students who pass the RSOS Class 10 examination can proceed with the admission process to Class 11 or equivalent courses. At the same time, the students who pass the RSOS Class 12 examination 2026 are now eligible to participate in the further admission process to their preferred undergraduate courses, diploma programmes and other higher education options.Details Mentioned on RSOS Result 2026 PDFThe students are advised to verify all the details mentioned in the marksheet carefully. In case of discrepancies, the students are required to reach out to the authorities at the earliest. The list of details is given below:Exam sessionClass (10th or 12th)Roll numberCandidate’s nameFather’s nameMother’s nameSubject codeTheory marksPractical marksSessional marksTotal marksAbbreviationsRajasthan Open Board Result (Pass/XXXX)Students can receive their original marksheet by reaching out to their respective schools. It should be noted that the digital marksheet released by the board is provisional in nature, however, it can be used by the students for further educational purposes.What to do if not satisfied with RSOS 2026 results?The students who are not satisfied with their RSOS 2026 results are advised to wait for the further information released by the RSOS in relation to the improvement opportunities and subsequent examination, which is the RSOS Supplementary examination. The students are therefore advised to check the official website of RSOS regularly to be updated with further supplementary exam related notifications.

title: Why Muslim Players Are Presented a Different POTM Award at FIFA World Cup 2026
description: FIFA has altered the Player of the Match (POTM) award presentation for Muslim players during the 2026 World Cup. Due to Islamic prohibitions on alcohol, the typical branding is removed when a Muslim player wins.
content: A significant modification to the Player of the Match (POTM) award presentation for Muslim players at the current FIFA World Cup 2026 has been implemented by the Fédération Internationale de Football Association (FIFA). This year's POTM award, which is sponsored by the beer company Michelob ULTRA, is decided by an online vote by fans. After the game, the winning player typically receives the trophy and poses for pictures against a backdrop that displays the sponsor's logo. Later, these pictures are posted on various FIFA World Cup social media platforms.Why Do Muslim Players Get Different POTM Awards?However, because drinking alcohol is forbidden in Islam and in a few nations with a majority of Muslims, FIFA has taken a distinct stance for Muslim players. The typical Michelob ULTRA branding is removed from the presentation backdrop when a Muslim player wins the POTM award.This particular adjustment was made Ismael Koné of Canada, Mahmoud Abunada of Qatar, Yan Diomande of the Ivory Coast, Ramin Rezaeian of Iran, Emam Ashour of Egypt, Ali Olwan of Jordan, Ismael Saibari of Morocco, and Johan Manzambi of Switzerland have all been adjusted.During their award presentations, the FIFA World Cup badge and the phrases "Superior Player of the Match" are displayed on the backdrop rather than Michelob ULTRA emblems. The Michelob ULTRA branding is not apparent, even though the trophy itself is almost unchanged.At the previous two FIFA World Cups, the beer corporation Budweiser was the official sponsor of the Player of the Match. Because the trophy was associated with an alcohol company, Egypt's custodian Mohamed El Shenawy famously refused to receive it following his outstanding performance against Uruguay during the 2018 tournament.In domestic football, similar adjustments have also been made. Yaya Touré was among the Muslim players who declined the Player of the Match award in the Premier League since it used to come with a bottle of alcohol. Since then, a more impartial prize has taken the place of the award, and trophy ceremonies now frequently use non-alcoholic champagne.

title: After Anthropic, Trump Administration Wants OpenAI To Limit Early GPT-5.6 Access Over National Security Concerns
description: The race to develop advanced AI models has seen increased government involvement, particularly from the US, which has reportedly asked OpenAI to delay the broad release of its GPT-5.6 model.
content: The race to build increasingly capable artificial intelligence models has entered a new phase and this time it is not just technology companies setting the pace. According to a Bloomberg report, the US government has reportedly asked ChatGPT maker OpenAI to slow the rollout of its upcoming GPT-5.6 model instead of releasing it broadly from day one. The reported request comes just weeks after Anthropic was forced to restrict access to its most advanced AI models following intervention from the Trump administration over national security concerns. The development suggests Washington is taking a more active role in shaping how the world's most advanced AI systems reach the public, particularly as concerns around security, misuse and foreign access continue to grow.OpenAI Reportedly Asked To Take A Phased ApproachAccording to Bloomberg, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman recently told employees that the Trump administration wants GPT-5.6 to be introduced in stages rather than launched immediately for everyone. The report says OpenAI has been asked to first make the model available to a limited group of trusted partners before expanding access to the wider public. During the internal meeting, Altman reportedly told employees that government officials have become increasingly cautious about the capabilities of frontier AI models.He also reportedly said the US-based AI giant should continue working with the administration on future safety measures and deployment restrictions, even if the company does not agree with every recommendation. Bloomberg noted that Altman did not specifically mention Anthropic while discussing the matter.Anthropic Case Casts A Long ShadowThe reported request follows the recent restrictions imposed on OpenAI rival Anthropic. Earlier this month, the AI giant suspended global access to its Mythos 5 and Fable 5 models after receiving government directives to restrict foreign nationals from accessing them, citing national security concerns. Anthropic has since been holding discussions with the White House in an attempt to restore broader access.Meanwhile, concerns over AI security have continued to escalate. A recently disclosed letter sent by Anthropic to US Senators Tim Scott and Elizabeth Warren alleged that Chinese tech giant Alibaba carried out a large-scale campaign to gain unauthorised access to Claude AI. According to the company, nearly 25,000 fraudulent accounts generated more than 28.8 million interactions with Claude between April 22 and June 5, 2026, in what it described as a coordinated distillation effort.According to people familiar with the matter cited by Bloomberg, OpenAI employees are now closely watching how the government's handling of Anthropic could influence the future release of GPT-5.6 and other advanced AI models. As regulatory scrutiny intensifies, the next generation of AI may face as many political hurdles as technical ones.

title: Apple Hikes MacBook, iPad Prices By 20-42%: Here's What Buyers Must Know
description: Apple has increased prices for its MacBooks, iPads, and other products in India due to rising memory chip costs.
content: Apple has increased prices of the MacBook and iPad from around 20-42 percent compared to their earlier launch prices. This move is across the globe, including in India due to the rising cost of memory chips. The new price list on Apple India's website shows that the company has increased the price of the MacBook Pro built on theM5 series chip by about 20 per cent. The price of 14-inch MacBook Pro with M5 Pro chip has been increased to Rs 2,99,900 apiece from the launch price of 2,49,900 per unit earlier. There has been a significant spike in the price of iPad Air. The basic model in 13-inch iPad Air has been increased by 41.22 percent to 1,19,900 apiece from Rs 84,900 a unit.Apple, in a statement, said that the consumer electronics industry is facing an unprecedented challenge, "the rapid expansion of AI data centres has created an extraordinary surge in demand for memory and storage. We have never seen a component price increase this much, this quickly. We have shielded our customers from these increases so far, but we have now reached a point where we need to begin raising prices on a number of products, including today's increases for iPad and Mac. We know this is not welcome news, and we are working tirelessly to find solutions," the statement said.Apple had earlier  in April stated, that earlier inventories had helped the firm keep its gross margins above Wall Street expectations, but that rising memory costs would start to catch up by the end of this month. "We expect significantly higher memory costs,” CEO Tim Cook had said on a conference call with analysts in April.Price rises  for consumer electronics are already widespread across the industry, with many products rising by 40 per cent or more.Memory companies have been allocating an increasing share of their production to AI data centres, leaving the consumer electronics segment with limited availability and rising costs..

title: Welcome To The Jungle OTT Release: Where To Watch Akshay Kumar's Comedy Film Online After Theatrical Run
description: Welcome To The Jungle, starring Akshay Kumar, Raveen Tandon, Suniel Shetty, and others, released in theatres on June 26, 2026. Here's where you can stream online after its theatrical run.
content: Welcome to the Jungle, the latest instalment in the hit Welcome franchise, finally arrived in theatres on June 26, bringing back the franchise's trademark blend of comedy, chaos, and star power. Headlined by Akshay Kumar and featuring an ensemble cast including Suniel Shetty, Paresh Rawal, Raveena Tandon, Jacqueline Fernandez, Disha Patani, Arshad Warsi, Sanjay Dutt, and others, the film has been one of the year's most anticipated Bollywood releases. With audiences now catching the film on the big screen, many are already eager to know where they can stream it after its theatrical run. Here's everything we know so far about Welcome to the Jungle's OTT release.Welcome To The Jungle OTT releaseWelcome to the Jungle will be available to stream on JioHotstar after completing its theatrical run. While the makers have not officially announced the OTT release date, the film's poster confirms the plotform as its streaming partner. Typically, Bollywood films arrive on OTT platforms around eight weeks after their theatrical release, suggesting the Akshay Kumar-led entertainer could premiere on that platform within that window.    The in-house review of Zoom says Welcome To The Jungle is best enjoyed with your expectations for logic left at the door. While it may not be flawless, the comedy delivers several hilarious moments that have the potential to become meme-worthy and earn cult-classic status over time.What's the plot of Welcome To The Jungle Welcome to the Jungle follows a corrupt politician-businessman who hatches an outrageous plan to launder Rs 2,000 crore of black money by producing the biggest box-office disaster ever made. Convinced that the film is bound to fail, he assembles a chaotic team comprising a washed-up superstar, an incompetent filmmaker, a gangster, and a group of eccentric personalities.However, things take an unexpected turn when the crew, shooting a military action film in a remote jungle village, is mistaken for a real army unit and finds itself caught in the middle of a genuine terrorist crisis. What begins as a bizarre money-laundering scheme soon transforms into a wildly unpredictable adventure packed with meta-humour, action, and absurd comedy.     Directed by Ahmed Khan and produced by Firoz A. Nadiadwala, the cast features Akshay Kumar as Rajeev, Arshad Warsi as Romeo, Paresh Rawal as Dr. Ghungroo, Suniel Shetty, Jackie Shroff, Raveena Tandon, Disha Patani, Jacqueline Fernandez, Lara Dutta, Johnny Lever, Rajpal Yadav, Shreyas Talpade, Tusshar Kapoor, and others. Welcome To The Jungle is running at a theatre near you!

title: Names Of Operation Sindoor Martyrs Made Public For First Time; Army War Memorial to Honour Them
description: For the first time, the names of six uniformed personnel who died in Operation Sindoor against Pakistan have been released, now listed on the Army War Memorial's website.
content: The names of six uniformed personnel who lost their lives during Operation Sindoor against Pakistan have been made public for the first time, with their details now featured on the Army War Memorial's official website. The list includes five Army personnel, namely Subedar Major Pawan Kumar, Rifleman Sunil Kumar, Lance Naik Dinesh Kumar, Agniveer Mood Murli Naik, and Havildar Sunil Kumar Singh, along with Sergeant Surendra Kumar of the Indian Air Force.The names have been included in the Army War Memorial's 2025 roll of honour, which records soldiers who laid down their lives in various military operations during the year. As part of the memorial's tradition, their names will be engraved on commemorative bricks at the National War Memorial in New Delhi.Two of the six have been conferred gallantry awards. Rifleman Sunil Kumar was awarded the Vir Chakra posthumously, while Sergeant Surendra Kumar received the Vayu Medal.President Droupadi Murmu presented the Vir Chakra (posthumously) to Rifleman Sunil Kumar's parents during the Defence Investiture Ceremony at Rashtrapati Bhavan on June 8.The publication of the names marks the first official disclosure of the identities of the six personnel killed during Operation Sindoor, India's military operation against Pakistan last year.Operation SindoorIndia and Pakistan were locked in a brief but intense military confrontation in May 2025, triggered by a terror attack in the Pahalgam valley of Jammu and Kashmir on April 22, which claimed 26 innocent lives. Following the terror attack, India responded with Operation Sindoor, targeting terror infrastructure in Pakistan and Pakistan-occupied Kashmir, and over 100 terrorists, including high-value targets like Yusuf Azhar, Abdul Malik Rauf, and Mudasir Ahmed, were successfully eliminated in the operation.New Delhi has maintained that, inflicted by this heavy damage, Pakistan's Director General of Military Operations (DGMO) called the Indian DGMO and both sides agreed to stop all firing and military action on land and in the air and sea with effect from May 10.

title: 'People Think My Life Is Set': Bihar Man Explains Why It's Just 'Average' In The US
description: An Indian man living in the US has gone viral after claiming that what many in Bihar consider a "successful" lifestyle is simply average in America. His comparison has sparked widespread discussion on quality of life and living standards.
content: A viral Instagram video by an Indian living in the United States has sparked a conversation about what qualifies as an "average" lifestyle in different parts of the world. In the clip, the Bihar native reflects on how moving abroad changed his perspective, arguing that many things considered signs of success in India are viewed as ordinary in the US.The video was posted by Arham Ishteyak, who compared his daily life in Bihar with his life in the United States. Sharing the clip, he wrote, "Average life in Bihar vs average life in the USA. Same person... completely different reality."In the video, Ishteyak said that people back home often assume he has "made it" because he owns the latest iPhone, travels frequently, wears good clothes, and has a stable job. However, he explained that these are not extraordinary achievements where he lives now but are part of what he considers an average lifestyle.        View this post on Instagram            A post shared by Arham Ishteyak (@arham_987)"But literally, this is the average life here, average life. This is the life of the average people here," he said. He added that there is still plenty of room for growth beyond that standard of living, but stressed that what many perceive as luxury is, in his experience, fairly commonplace in the US.The video resonated with many social media users, especially members of the Indian diaspora, and drew a wave of reactions in the comments section. One user wrote, "As conscious citizens, we need to focus on the quality of life and social development rather than casteism and divisions in society." Another simply commented, "Keep growing, bro."

title: Khatron Ke Khiladi Season 15 Premiere Date: When And Where To Watch Rohit Shetty-Hosted Show
description: After a long wait, Rohit Shetty-hosted Khatron Ke Khiladi 15 will be premiering on Colors in July. The show goes on air after Laughter Chefs season 3 wraps.
content: The 15th season of Rohit Shetty’s adventure-based reality show, Khatron Ke Khiladi, has been filmed and is ready for a grand premiere. Popular celebrities from the television industry will be seen participating in stunts. The annual show skipped its season in 2025 and after a two-year-long wait, fans will finally get to watch some jaw-dropping and adrenaline-pumping activities. Khatron Ke Khiladi 15’s premiere date has been locked and announced. The show will go on air only after the celebrity cooking reality program, Laughter Chefs 3, comes to an end.Khatron Ke Khiladi 15 premiere date and time Khatron Ke Khiladi 15 premieres on Colors on July 25. It is a biweekly show, airing every Saturday and Sunday at 9 PM. A new promo, offering a glimpse of what’s in store this season, was unveiled as well.          View this post on Instagram            A post shared by Farrhana Bhatt (@farrhana_bhatt) Khatron Ke Khiladi 15 contestantsThis season has Gaurav Khanna, Rubina Dilaik, Avinash Mishra, Orhan Awatramani, Harsh Gujral, Farrhana Bhatt, Shagun Sharma, Avika Gor, Jasmin Bhasin, Karan Wahi, Rithvik Dhanjani, and Vishal Aditya Singh participating as contestants. Shot in Cape Town, Khatron Ke Khiladi 15 has a unique format as it invites former contestants as well. Why was Khatron Ke Khiladi 15 delayedKhatron Ke Khiladi 15 was scheduled to go on floors by mid-2025. But, in April 2025, Banijay Asia’s exit as the producer delayed the show. According to reports, the production house that backed Khatron Ke Khiladi and Bigg Boss ended its contract with Colors channel. Some participants were already locked for the stunt-based reality show. Following the internal shuffle, its future was uncertain. A source close to the production house revealed that Bigg Boss 19 and Khatron Ke Khiladi 15 would move from Colors and air on Sony TV. However, the shows have been retained on the former channel. While Bigg Boss 19 was backed by Banijay Asia, Khatron Ke Khiladi 15 is also on track once again. Internal conflicts between Banijay Asia and Endemol were said to be the root cause of the delay. By mid-2025, Colors had reportedly started looking for another production house to back the show. The matter has now been resolved. Khatron Ke Khiladi 15 finalistsAs per early reports, Farrhana and Karan Wahi are the top two contestants. Avinash Mishra, Ruhaanika Dhawan and Rithvik Dhanjani are reportedly in the top five. For the first time in the show’s history, former contestants who did not win the trophy, will be contesting with the new participants.

title: Iran US Israel war Live Updates: Cargo Ship Hit Near Oman, IMO Suspends Evacuation of Vessels
description: Days after US-Iran Switzerland talks, Pakistan on Thursday said that talks between the US and Iran are expected to resume next week. In a statement issued late on Wednesday, Pakistan's foreign office spokesperson Tahir Andrabi said “parties are on the table” and that the process was going on. The US and Iran last week signed a memorandum of understanding aimed at restoring peace in West Asia.Earlier this week, they engaged in technical-level talks in Switzerland's Burgenstock, in the presence of mediators Pakistan, which has signed the memorandum as a “guarantor”, and Qatar, following which they agreed on a roadmap towards a final peace deal in 60 days.“Talks are continuing. I believe that the talks will resume next week, probably on Tuesday... Basically, this is a temporary gap, and it is not as such a break,” Andrabi was quoted as saying in the statement.“Our delegation was there in Burgenstock for the 22nd June talks. I understand that our delegation will also be there when the talks resume next week,” he added.He also said the expected resumption of talks between the US and Iran next week was a "positive development".However, the US and Iran have not yet said anything on the possible resumption of talks.
content: Days after US-Iran Switzerland talks, Pakistan on Thursday said that talks between the US and Iran are expected to resume next week. In a statement issued late on Wednesday, Pakistan's foreign office spokesperson Tahir Andrabi said “parties are on the table” and that the process was going on. title: Siya's Parents Demand Harshest Punishment If Guilty: 'Should Be Thrown From...'
description: Siya Goyal's parents demanded the harshest punishment for their daughter if found guilty in the alleged murder of her fiancé Ketan Agarwal, saying she should be "thrown from the same place" where he died at Lohagad Fort.
content: Pune: In a startling turn in the Lohagad Fort murder case, the parents of prime accused Siya Goyal have demanded that their own daughter be punished in the exact manner the victim died, if she is found guilty of conspiring to kill her fiancé, Ketan Agarwal.Speaking to reporters, as per ANI, Siya's mother, Pooja Goyal, said: "If anyone is at fault in this, they should be given the harshest punishment. Even if it includes my daughter. If my daughter is found guilty, then she should be thrown from the same place where Ketan was thrown... The family had so much hope for Siya and Ketan's marriage. There were so many expectations, so much affection. Ketan's family also treated Siya with great affection and love. All the functions were going well. They had also organised many programs for her birthday celebration. If we felt that Siya was having some problem or was not able to talk to Ketan, then they would discuss with us... Siya never expressed any displeasure about this marriage... We did not know anything about Chetan. Ever since the engagement, she only spoke to Ketan... I don't believe that she agreed (to commit the crime), but if the investigation reveals she is guilty, then give her the harshest punishment... For the marriage, they booked Ananta Resort in Udaipur. The cost was Rs 3 crores, and we had to spend Rs 5 crores on the wedding..."        Agarwal, a 25-year-old real estate firm director, was initially believed to have died in an accidental fall into a gorge at Pune's Lohagad Fort. However, the investigation took a dramatic turn after police uncovered evidence of an alleged murder conspiracy, leading to the arrest of his fiancée, Siya, and her boyfriend, Chetan Chaudhary.'Would A 19-Year-Old Girl Do Such A Thing?'Siya's father, Pravin Goyal, echoed the same demand, telling reporters: "Whoever did this should receive the harshest punishment. Just like the fort from which our Ketan fell, whoever is found guilty, even if it's my own daughter, should be pushed from there..."He added: "We did not have any prior knowledge about Siya's love affairs. Siya was a simple girl. She is 19 years old. Would a 19-year-old girl do such a thing? I don't know anything about him. Where did Chetan come from? I've never seen his face, nor have I ever spoken to him in my life. He has never been to our house. Ketan's family members are very good, cultured people... We've lost a son and a wonderful relationship... There will be an investigation. Let the investigation take place. I would only want that whatever result comes out, work should be done on it, and the guilty person should be punished..."        Meanwhile, Pune Rural Police investigating the case have alleged that Siya did not want to marry Ketan and conspired with her friend Chetan Chaudhary to eliminate him. Police said the two first met at a Diwali party last year and grew close over the following months, remaining in regular contact while allegedly discussing plans to kill Ketan. Investigators suspect Siya wanted more time for herself and was unwilling to proceed with the marriage despite family pressure, and that while she had grown close to Chetan, she was reportedly not ready to marry him either, allegedly wanting to eliminate Ketan while continuing her relationship with Chetan.According to the probe, Siya allegedly first thought of killing Ketan after seeing him seated near the edge of Lohagad Fort during a visit on 31 May. Police suspect an earlier attempt was made on 14 June, when Siya allegedly tried to push him off the fort; when that attempt failed, she reportedly raised a false alarm about spotting a snake and later claimed the push had been accidental.Ketan's uncle, Vijay Agarwal, demanded justice for his nephew, telling reporters: "We want justice. The search is ongoing, and we have been told it continues, but we have no further information... My son was 26, well-educated. Listening to his parents, he agreed to an arranged marriage, and he was killed. Who said what, I do not know. I only believe in our country's law..."Both Siya Goyal and Chetan Chaudhary remain in police custody till 29 June as the investigation continues.

title: Deal in Danger: Iran Attacks Cargo Ship in Strait of Hormuz Days After Truce
description: This incident raises concerns about Iran's commitment to the agreement, which includes the promise to facilitate commercial vessel passage in exchange for lifting US sanctions.
content: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) attacked a Singapore-flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday, according to two senior US officials cited by The Wall Street Journal. The attack comes less than a week after the United States and Iran agreed a deal aimed at ending hostilities and reopening the shipping route. The vessel, Ever Lovely, sustained damage to its bridge but there were no casualties, according to the UK Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO). The incident occurred near the coast of Oman, hours after the IRGC's navy warned ships against using routes through the strait that had not been approved by Iran. The attack is likely to raise questions about the implementation of the 60-day agreement under which Iran committed to making its "best efforts" to ensure the safe passage of commercial vessels through the waterway. In return, the US agreed to lift its blockade of Iranian ports.As part of the deal, Washington also waived sanctions on Iranian oil sales and allowed Tehran to sell crude oil in US dollars for the first time in decades. Shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz had begun to recover this week after months of disruption.      https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Vt9uBTtfEEQAccording to ship-tracking firm Kpler, 70 vessels crossed the waterway on Wednesday, more than double the number recorded the previous day. The company said the increase suggested shipping operators were regaining confidence in using the route. The Strait of Hormuz, a critical global energy chokepoint, had seen only limited traffic in recent months because of concerns over Iranian attacks and the US blockade. Over the weekend, Iran declared the strait closed again following fighting between Israel and the Iran-backed Hezbollah group in Lebanon. Ending that conflict is one of the conditions of the broader agreement intended to halt hostilities between the US and Iran. Iran has not officially confirmed that the waterway has reopened. However, it had not attacked commercial vessels transiting the strait since 12 June, when a tanker was struck days before the interim agreement was signed. On Thursday, the Revolutionary Guard said on its official Telegram channel that three tankers using the southern route endorsed by the International Maritime Organization (IMO) had been ordered to turn back. Maritime intelligence firm Windward said five vessels made U-turns. The Revolutionary Guard had earlier warned that any attempt to use a route designated by the IMO would be "unacceptable and completely dangerous" and said all vessels should coordinate with Iranian authorities. According to ship-tracking service Marine Traffic, Ever Lovely had loaded cargo at Umm Qasr in Iraq and was bound for Singapore. The vessel's owner, Evergreen Marine Asia Pte Ltd in Singapore, could not immediately be reached for comment. Financial data provider LSEG said the ship had been stranded in the Gulf for more than 100 days before departing towards the mouth of the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday morning. It was one of four vessels attempting to transit the waterway at around the same time. Ship-tracking data and crew members aboard a nearby vessel indicated that all four ships were following a route identified by the IMO, hugging the Omani coastline. Ever Lovely was travelling fastest and was leading the group. According to seafarers in the flotilla, no warning was issued by the Iranian navy over radio communications and the ships were not instructed to turn back before the attack.

title: The Living Statue: Meet Michel Nkuka Mboladinga, Face Of DR Congo's WC Campaign
description: Michel Nkuka Mboladinga has become the face of DR Congo's FIFA World Cup campaign. The Congolese fan stands motionless for 90 minutes with his right hand raised as a tribute to the late Patrice Lumumba.
content: While the FIFA World Cup is full of stories of teams and players fighting the odds and making it to the biggest stages, in DR Congo's case, a fan has caught the limelight despite their draw against Cristiano Ronaldo-starrer Portugal. The tale of Michel Nkuka Mboladinga is fascinating, as the 'living statue' stands still in the stands and appears as if there is an idol placed there and not a real human being.Michel stands frozen in the stands for the entire duration of Congo's matches during the tournament. He stands motionless with his right hand raised for 90 minutes and has become an internet sensation after first going viral during the Africa Cup of Nations earlier this year as he appears dressed in colourful suits. The Story Behind The Living Statue Michel is nicknamed 'Lumumba Vea' by Congo fans, and his appearance is a tribute to the late Congolese leader Patrice Lumumba. In fact, his standing posture is similar to his statue in Kinshasa. Lumumba was the first-ever prime minister of independent Congo in 1960. Lumumba was among the most influential personalities in the country's independent movement after earlier being a colony of Belgium. He was assassinated in 1961 by separatists in the Katanga region.Over the years, his death has become a source of major controversy, with foreign powers alleged to be involved. In 2002, the Belgian foreign ministry read an apology in the parliament claiming 'moral responsibility.' In 2022, the Belgian prime minister Alexander De Croo apologised to Lumumba's family and returned his gold-capped tooth, which was the only part of his body that remained. “I stand motionless because I believe it gives the team emotional stamina. Just as Lumumba sacrificed his life for our country, mine is a small price to pay because of how deeply I care about this team," said Michel to the Wall Street Journal.How Does He Stand Motionless For 90 Minutes?Michel revealed that standing motionless with his arm raised for 90 minutes does not come easily, and he practices for it for over 20 days a month. However, he also takes sufficient rest and claims the weather does not impact him. “Believe it or not, but I do practice. I can actually practice 20 days out of a month, but I’ll also take a lot of rest. I am a living statue. The climate has no impact on me. My job is not just to stand there, but rather to communicate energy, strength and power to the players. That is what I am focusing on. I do not foresee a time when I’m actually going to let go and lower my hand – I will get my job done," said Michel to CNN Sports. Forced To Miss Historic Portugal Clash However, the living statue was missing from the stands as Congo created history and held Portugal to a 1-1 draw in their opening game of the World Cup due to quarantine restrictions forced by the Ebola outbreak in Congo. The players of the Congo squad played a key role in ensuring that Michel was able to reach North America for the mega event and even pleaded with the country's president Félix Tshisekedi to include him in the official delegation. Michel was also forced to miss Congo's World Cup qualifier in Jamaica earlier this year, where the team sealed their place in the World Cup after 52 years due to visa issues. However, he finally appeared in the World Cup during Congo's second match against Colombia, which they lost 1-0. He is expected to be present when they face Uzbekistan on Saturday (June 27) in a game where a victory would seal a place in the knockouts for the first time in their history.

title: The Legal Cost Of A Missing Expiry Date In India’s Digital Marketplace
description: Deceiving to sell: Missing expiry-date disclosures on online grocery platforms may be more than a compliance lapse. Existing laws mandate such information, exposing e-comm companies to potential consumer complaints and regulatory scrutiny.
content: Deceiving to sell: The law on this is not new, and it is not unclear. Yet a recent survey has found that nearly half of online grocery shoppers in India cannot locate expiry or best-before information before completing a purchase, with several major quick-commerce platforms found inconsistent in displaying this data while only a handful remained compliant. This is not a minor lapse in user experience. It is a legal failure, and the distinction matters because one invites an apology while the other invites liability.The Legal Metrology Act, 2009, read with the Legal Metrology (Packaged Commodities) Rules, 2011, requires every packaged commodity to disclose its date of manufacture, net quantity, retail price, and best-before or use-by date. This requirement was extended explicitly to e-commerce through the 2017 amendment to these Rules. A platform selling a packaged product online cannot treat itself as exempt from disclosures that a brick-and-mortar retailer must make on the physical package. The obligation is to display this information at the point of digital sale, on the screen, before payment is made, not to leave it buried in fine print or discoverable only after the package physically arrives at the consumer's door.Layered on top of this is the Consumer Protection Act, 2019, and the Consumer Protection (E-Commerce) Rules, 2020, framed under it. These Rules distinguish marketplace entities, which merely host sellers, from inventory entities, which sell directly to consumers. That distinction was never intended to operate as an exemption from disclosure. Sellers operating on a marketplace are required to disclose the country of origin and the expiry date among other particulars, and the marketplace itself is obligated to ensure that this information is displayed clearly and accessibly. Where a marketplace fails to exercise this due diligence consistently, it risks losing the safe-harbour protection ordinarily available to intermediaries under Section 79 of the Information Technology Act, 2000. A platform that actively ranks, recommends, and curates listings cannot simultaneously claim to be a passive conduit when it withholds the one disclosure that would materially affect a buyer's decision.A further complication lies in how this issue is being handled at the regulatory level itself. When the absence of expiry information online was escalated, the food safety regulator's response was that such declarations fall under the Legal Metrology framework administered by the Department of Consumer Affairs, and not within its own jurisdiction. This kind of jurisdictional clarification, while technically accurate, offers little to a consumer facing a product listing with no visible shelf-life information. The law does not contemplate a regulatory vacuum on this question, and two regulators each directing complaints to the other is itself a compliance gap. The Department of Consumer Affairs, as the rule-making authority, is best placed to close this through clearer enforcement guidance and coordinated action with sectoral regulators.A more recent regulatory development offers a useful template for a solution. The government has moved from purely disclosure-based obligations toward what may be termed architecture-based obligations, requiring that certain information be not merely present on a listing but searchable and filterable, so that a consumer can sort and screen products by relevant criteria, such as country of origin, before clicking through to a product page. This is a meaningful evolution in regulatory thinking, and it should logically extend to expiry and manufacturing dates as well. A consumer ought to be able to filter grocery listings by remaining shelf life, rather than rely on locating a date stamp buried somewhere within a listing.The consumer's right to be informed is not a courtesy extended by platforms at their discretion. It is a statutory right under the Consumer Protection Act, 2019, and its breach, where it causes loss or harm including the sale of near-expiry or expired goods, can found a complaint before the consumer commissions. Platforms can no longer rely on the claim that they are mere intermediaries when they actively curate and promote the very listings whose mandatory particulars they fail to disclose. The fall-back liability provisions under the E-Commerce Rules already contemplate platform responsibility where a seller's failure causes consumer loss, and a pattern of non-disclosure across thousands of listings reflects platform-level systemic failure rather than isolated seller default.What this calls for is not new legislation. The existing framework is detailed and has been refined through successive amendments specifically to keep pace with the shift to digital retail. What is missing is consistent enforcement, and a recognition by platforms that these disclosures are binding legal obligations rather than optional design choices. As online retail continues to expand its share of India's everyday commerce, the gap between what the law requires and what the screen shows is not a technical oversight. It is a legal exposure, and platforms that close this gap voluntarily will spare themselves the considerably more expensive experience of having it closed for them by a consumer commission.(Sonam Chandwani is the Managing Partner at KS Legal & Associates and heads the firm's Corporate Litigation Practice.)

title: Who Is Mahesh Dixit, Intelligence Veteran Appointed New IB Chief
description: The government has appointed Mahesh Dixit, a Special Director in the Intelligence Bureau, as the new Director of the agency, succeeding Tapan Kumar Deka.
content: Mahesh Dixit, 1993-batch IPS officer and intelligence veteran, has been appointed as India's next Intelligence Bureau (IB) chief. He will replace IPS Tapan Kumar Deka, who has been heading the intelligence bureau department since last four years. Dixit is currently serving as Special Director, Intelligence Bureau (IB). The Appointments Committee of the Cabinet (ACC), chaired by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, approved the appointment of Dixit, currently the second in command in the IB, for a period of two years.Dixit, a 1993-batch IPS officer of the Andhra Pradesh cadre will take over the incumbent IB Director Tapan Kumar Deka, a 1988-batch IPS officer of the Himachal Pradesh cadre. Deka is nearing completion of his second extended term that is set to mature on June 30.According to an order by the Ministry of Personnel, Dixit has been appointed as Director of the Intelligence Bureau for a tenure of two years from the date he assumes charge of the post or until further orders, whichever is earlier.The government has granted him an extension in service under the provisions of Fundamental Rule (FR) 56(d) and Rule 16(1A) of the All India Services (Death-cum-Retirement Benefits) Rules, 1958, to enable him to serve in the top intelligence position."The Appointments Committee of the Cabinet has approved the appointment of Mahesh Dixit, IPS (Arunachal Pradesh:93 batch), Special Director, Intelligence Bureau as Director, Intelligence Bureau vice Tapan Kumar Deka, IPS (Himachal Pradesh:88 batch), for a tenure of two years from the date of assumption of the charge of the post or until further orders, whichever is earlier, by granting extension in service under the provisions of FR 56(d) and Rule 16 (1A) of All India Services (Death cum Retirement Benefits) Rules, 1958," reads the order.Who Is Mahesh Dixit?Mahesh Dixit is a seasoned intelligence officer with extensive experience in counter-terrorism, internal security and intelligence operations.Dixit Served As Head of J&K Intelligence Unit Before being posted as Special Director at the IB headquarters in New Delhi, he headed the Subsidiary Intelligence Bureau (SIB) in Jammu and Kashmir, one of the agency's most crucial operational assignments given the region's security challenges.Officials credit him with playing a pivotal role in maintaining internal stability in the aftermath of the abrogation of Article 370.Dixit was also instrumental in breaking a major "white-collar" terror network last year, executing the operation after developing an initial lead provided by the Srinagar police.In 2025, Dixit was elevated when he was moved from Jammu and Kashmir to the Intelligence Bureau headquarters and appointed as agency's second-in-command.Dixit Focused on Counter-Terrorism Ops, Intelligence Coordination During his tenure so far in the intelligence bureau, the department focused on counter-terrorism operations, intelligence coordination and emerging security threats.With Dixit's appointment, the government has entrusted an experienced intelligence hand with the responsibility of heading the country's oldest and most important internal intelligence agency.Meanwhile, outgoing Director Tapan Deka has led the agency since July 2022. He had received successive two service extensions from the government, reflecting the importance attached to continuity in the country's intelligence apparatus.With inputs from ANI, PTI

title: Venezuela Earthquake Horror: Survivors Describe 'Ground Moving Like Sea'
description: On Wednesday, Venezuela experienced two major earthquakes, measuring magnitudes of 7.2 and 7.5, leading to significant destruction from La Guaira to Caracas. Panic ensued among residents, many of whom spent the night outdoors
content: Two powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela in quick succession on Wednesday, causing widespread destruction from the coastal state of La Guaira to the capital, Caracas. As of Thursday, at least 235 people had been killed and thousands more injured after the earthquakes, which measured magnitudes of 7.2 and 7.5. Buildings collapsed as the ground shook violently, trapping people beneath rubble and prompting large-scale rescue operations. Residents Describe Panic People across the Caracas metropolitan area described scenes of fear as the earthquakes struck and aftershocks continued through the night. "I was at home, and the house began shaking violently. At first, I thought it was the wind coming through the windows, but when I looked outside and saw that it was shaking too, I grabbed onto the doorway and started to pray," 52-year-old Maria Gonzalez told Al Jazeera. Gonzalez, who lives in Chacao on the outskirts of Caracas, said several items fell inside her home during the earthquake. Although her apartment building was not damaged, she chose to remain in Plaza Altamira, an open public space, as more than 10 aftershocks rattled the city. "I stayed out on the street until three in the morning because I would leave my home whenever aftershocks hit. People were having nervous breakdowns and crying," she said.            https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=joZEs2qQUxA Marian Rieber, a health journalist in Caracas, told Newsweek she was taking her dog downstairs when the earthquake struck. "At 6:05 in the afternoon, I went to take the dog down the stairs, floor two, and when I went down, everything began to move from left to right very strongly as if it were the tide of a beach," she said. "But we were on the concrete." She described the sound as "deafening", saying metal, glass and concrete groaned simultaneously while neighbours screamed. "This was very long, very long," she said. "I was attached to my dog, and I felt that the floor kept moving, then the aftershocks came." With mobile phone service disrupted in parts of Caracas, many residents spent the night outdoors. "Most Venezuelans have slept in the car doing vigils, taking care of each other," Rieber said. "Taking the dogs where we go... unfortunately, this is a tragedy, to which we are not used and we are not prepared."        Altamira and Los Palos Grandes Altamira was among the worst-affected areas of Caracas. According to AFP, a 22-storey building collapsed completely in the neighbourhood. Residents described clouds of dust rising between buildings as volunteers searched through the rubble with flashlights while people called out the names of missing relatives. A Guardian reporter said at least three buildings had collapsed in Altamira, with some people believed trapped beneath the debris. Outside one building, a person was seen weeping and calling for a grandmother feared to be inside while emergency workers and volunteers searched for survivors. Three people were killed in the Baruta district after two buildings collapsed, according to the district's mayor, Reuters reported.            https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QgDUkd0h-1gThe mayor of Chacao said one person had died, four buildings had collapsed and 22 people had been taken to hospital. The JW Marriott hotel, which has served as the United States' unofficial embassy since January's military intervention, was also badly shaken. Videos posted on social media appeared to show guests fleeing as the building shook. La Guaira and Catia La Mar La Guaira, home to the country's main international airport, also suffered extensive damage. The airport sustained what authorities described as "severe damage", with sections of the roof collapsing onto passengers attempting to flee. Acting President Delcy Rodríguez said the airport was closed immediately after the earthquake. Metro and rail services were also suspended.        Imágenes aéreas de La Guaira. Terremoto en #Venezuela. No para de sorprender tanta tragedia. 🚨🆘‼️ pic.twitter.com/8tV85HNrle— Cristian Crespo F. 🇨🇺 (@cristiancrespoj) June 25, 2026She said rescue teams from other parts of the country were being deployed to La Guaira. Nearby Catia La Mar was also heavily affected. Reports indicated that more than a dozen large buildings, including beachfront hotels and residential towers, had collapsed, with the Playa Grande neighbourhood among the hardest hit. "Dozens of buildings have collapsed there … and we are currently carrying out intensive rescue operations to save lives," Rodríguez said. Damage beyond the Capital The epicentre of the earthquakes was located west of Caracas near the coastal town of Morón in Carabobo state. In Naguanagua, several building walls collapsed, while residents in Valencia, the state capital, gathered outside damaged buildings amid fears of further aftershocks. Emergency crews continued rescue operations across affected areas as authorities assessed the full scale of the destruction.

title: Ecuador Coach Climbs Stands to Celebrate Upset Over Germany - WATCH
description: Ecuador achieved a stunning 2-1 victory over Germany in a crucial FIFA World Cup match, ensuring their progression to the knockout stage. This win marks a significant upset in the tournament, although Germany still topped Group E.
content: La Tri shocked four-time winners Germany with a 2-1 victory in New York New Jersey Stadium in the USA on Friday, ensuring Ecuador's survival in the FIFA World Cup 2026. Leroy Sane gave Germany the lead early in the crucial match, but goals from Angulo and Plata helped Ecuador make a remarkable comeback in the match. Ecuador coach Sebastian Beccacece jumped and climbed on to the stand to embrace his family after Plata finished La Tri's comeback against the Mannaschaft.Sebastian Beccacece's CelebrationsSebastian Beccacece marked the upset over Germany with an unforgettable World Cup celebration. As one of the top eight third-place team, La Tri's 2-1 victory in New Jersey ensures their position in the round of 32. At the last whistle, manager Beccacece just went crazy. After celebrating with his staff in a huddle, he headed straight for the seats behind the dugout and started climbing the stand.Beccacece scaled a barrier where his loved ones seemed to be seated, gave them a group hug, and held on to them tightly. While the Ecuadorian players celebrated together, fans in other parts of the MetLife were shedding happy tears, with some still displaying symptoms of utter shock. Chelsea star Moises Caicedo lied flat on his back before rising to his knees and pointed up into the sky.Germany Take Early LeadDespite the loss, Germany's two victories over Curacao and Ivory Coast in their first two games helped them top Group E with six points.Leroy Sane gave Germany the lead in just the second minute of the game. After a move that caused controversy because of Aleksandar Pavlovic's lifted boot during the build-up, the winger composedly reached the back of the net. The four-time world champions gained an early lead as the referee upheld the goal in spite of the objections.Ecuador's ComebackEcuador reacted quickly, leveling the score in just seven minutes. La Tri regained parity with a fantastic long-range goal from Jhonny Angulo, who cut in from the left and past Manuel Neuer from outside the penalty area.The teams were tied 1-1 going into halftime with everything still up for grabs in an important Group E match. Gonzalo Plata took advantage of a well-worked corner that was sent to the near post in the 77th minute, which proved to be the game-changing moment. The Ecuadorian supporters were ecstatic when Plata touched the ball before Neuer and poked it into the net.Ecuador secured a historic victory over Germany by remaining resolute for the rest of the game. The outcome is the biggest upset of the competition thus far and guaranteed their spot in the knockout stages while maintaining their World Cup campaign. Germany nevertheless won Group E in spite of the loss.

title: Passport-Citizenship Row: Govt Asserts No New Decision In Past 12 Yrs
description: A controversy has arisen regarding the proof of Indian citizenship, sparked by Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) officials stating that a passport is merely a travel document, not definitive proof of citizenship.
content: A new debate has erupted in the country that is centered around the question - what proves a person's citizenship after reports surfaced quoting Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) officials that an Indian passport is not a proof of one's citizenship as it identifies only as a travel document. "It was not decided yesterday that the passport is not a proof of citizenship. It was not even decided in the last 12 years under the Narendra Modi government. The passport has never been a proof of citizenship," a government source said.Citing Section 20 of the Passports Act, 1967, it says that passports and travel documents can be issued to persons who are not citizens of India. The sources also cited Section 20 of the Passports Act, 1967, which says that passports and travel documents can be issued to persons who are not citizens of India."Notwithstanding anything contained in the foregoing provisions relating to the issue of a passport or travel document, the central government may issue, or cause to be issued, a passport or travel document to a person who is not a citizen of India if that government is of the opinion that it is necessary so to do in the public interest," it said.However, Section 6(2)(a) of the same Passports Act, 1967 provides that the passport authority shall refuse to issue a passport "if the applicant is not a citizen of India".This erupted a raging controversy on the passport-citizenship issue with the government asserting that no new decision has been taken on the travel document in the past 12 years while the opposition alleged that groundwork is being done to "arbitrarily deny" citizenship rights to those who "disagree" with the ruling dispensation.Citing the Passport Act 1967, government sources said passports can be given to even non-citizens "in the public interest" while Election Commission officials said the passport continues to be among the 12 valid supporting documents required by voters to prove their eligibility to be on the voters' list.https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zkTENDftmocOppn Alleges Centre Laying Groundwork For Denying Citizenship As the matter was highlighted, the Congress party alleged that the government is laying the groundwork to arbitrarily deny citizenship rights to Indians with whom it disagrees.Congress doubled down on its attack on the government with party's general secretary (organisation) KC Venugopal alleging that the BJP government had earlier removed the names of people from certain communities from the electoral rolls to suit its political agenda."This government excels in creating panic and helplessness among ordinary citizens. By declaring that even a passport doesn't certify one's citizenship, they are laying the groundwork to arbitrarily deny citizenship rights to Indians with whom they disagree," Venugopal said in a post on X.BJP Says No New Decision By Govt on Passport-Citizenship Defending MEA's statement, the BJP asserted that the government did not bring or introduce any new rule or addition. It stated that the MEA has merely reiterated a long-settled legal position that a passport alone is not conclusive proof of Indian citizenship.BJP Cites Passport Act 1967The BJP cited the Passports Act, 1967, and judicial rulings, including a 2013 Bombay High Court judgment, to contend that citizenship is determined under the Citizenship Act, 1955, and not by the possession of any single document.https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aqOCH3iw4bcBJP Hits Out At 'Kagaz Nahin Dikhayenge' Brigade Accusing the opposition of sensationalising a settled legal position for political gains, BJP IT department head Amit Malviya attacked the opposition, describing them as the "kagaz nahin dikhayenge" brigade.Poll Body Says Passport Remains Valid Document For Eligibility In SIRAmid the debate and concerns being raised on what accounts for a person's Indian citizenship, the EC officials said that Indian passports continue to be among the 12 valid supporting documents required by voters to prove their eligibility to be on the voters' list under the ongoing special intensive revision of electoral rolls.During the EC conducted Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls in Bihar and the special revision in Assam, and subsequent phases of poll roll revision, passports have consistently been listed as one of the 12 documents that individuals can submit when applying to register or maintain their status on the electoral rolls."Passport was and continues to be one of the documents to establish identity," an official said, underlining that "there is no change". The electoral registration officer examines one of the indicative documents to decide whether a person is eligible to be on the voters' list.Now Passport, Earlier Aadhaar - Both No Proof of Citizenship During the hearing on the SIR, the Supreme Court had observed that Aadhaar was not conclusive proof of citizenship, just a document of identity.Which Document is a Proof of Citizenship of India The Press Information Bureau (PIB), on December 20, 2019, issued a comprehensive explainer via a set of Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) regarding the National Register of Citizens (NRC) and the Citizenship Amendment Act (CAA).It said, "Citizenship can be proved by submitting any documents related to date of birth and place of birth. However, a decision is yet to be taken on such acceptable documents."https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sBrUIYL4n_sCitizenship Rules Based on Citizenship Act, 1955The PIB also said in the FAQs that citizenship of any person is decided on the basis of the Citizenship Rules, 2009. These rules are based on the Citizenship Act, 1955.It said five ways for any person to become a citizen of India were citizenship by birth, citizenship by descent, citizenship by registration, citizenship by naturalisation, and citizenship by incorporation.TMC's Mahua Moitra Takes on GovtTMC leader and Lok Sabha MP Mahua Moitra took a dig at the government over the issue. "It would seem that the only proof of Indian citizenship today is to be both Hindu and a BJP voter. Nothing else will do," she said on X.Owaisi Pitches In, Taunts Govt on Citizenship Row AIMIM chief and Lok Sabha MP from Hyderabad, Asaduddin Owaisi asked if passport, birth certificate, Aadhaar card, and voter ID card are not documents of citizenship, then which document is required to prove citizenship."According to the government, no document is conclusive proof of citizenship. By 2030, only one document will be proof of citizenship: BJP membership," he said.Over to Supreme Court, Says Kapil Sibal Rajya Sabha member Kapil Sibal also hit out at the government over the matter. "Which document then is proof of citizenship? BLO can doubt my citizenship. Deprive me of my vote. Result: BJP wins the election. Over to Supreme Court!" he said on X.Lyricist Javed Akhtar termed the MEA clarification "absurd"."The ministry of external affairs says that a passport is a document of travel, not the proof of citizenship. Really??? So are they providing this travel document to some people without being totally convinced that this person is an Indian citizen? It is absurd," he said on X.With inputs from PTI

title: Himalayas Are India's Biggest Earthquake Threat—Here's How Scientists Are Prepar...
description: India has developed a regional earthquake early warning (EEW) system capable of providing a few seconds of advance notice after a quake begins.
content: As powerful earthquakes continue to strike different parts of the world, attention has once again turned to whether countries can warn people before devastating tremors arrive. In India, scientists stress there is no scientifically proven technology capable of predicting earthquakes before they occur. However, the country has made significant progress in earthquake early warning (EEW) technology that can provide a few crucial seconds of notice after a quake has already begun.India's regional earthquake early warning system has been developed primarily for the seismically active Himalayan belt, where tectonic activity poses one of the country's greatest natural disaster risks. The initiative combines dense seismic sensor networks, rapid data processing and mobile alert systems designed to reduce casualties during major earthquakes.The system does not predict earthquakes days or hours in advance. Instead, it detects the earliest seismic waves immediately after an earthquake begins and sends warnings before the stronger, more destructive shaking reaches nearby cities.How India's Earthquake Early Warning System WorksThe country's most advanced EEW programme has been developed by IIT Roorkee in collaboration with the Government of Uttarakhand, which also introduced the BhuDEV mobile application to deliver real-time alerts to residents.
